question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Medium: Impacts caused by griefing with no economic damage to any user on the network'] [Function: execute()] Can an unprivileged external user repeatedly submit requests with a zero receiver address (triggering `isContractCreate = true`) and arbitrarily large `callData` via `params.getCallData()` to force the node into expensive `buildContractCreateTransactionBody()` execution paths, causing CPU/memory resource exhaustion that degrades service availability for all other users without any economic cost to the attacker?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Medium: Impacts caused by griefing with no economic damage to any user on the network'] [Function: buildEthereumTransactionBody()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a `ContractDebugParameters` request with `ethereumData` set to a maximally-sized byte array, causing `Bytes.wrap(params.getEthereumData())` to allocate a large buffer and `maxGasAllowance(Long.MAX_VALUE)` to be set, resulting in disproportionate resource consumption in the executor and griefing other concurrent users?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Medium: Impacts caused by griefing with no economic damage to any user on the network'] [Function: patchSenderNonce()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a `ContractDebugParameters` with a valid sender address and an `ethereumData` payload whose decoded nonce (via `populateEthTxData(params.getEthereumData()).nonce()`) differs from the stored `account.ethereumNonce()`, causing `writableCache.put()` to silently overwrite the in-memory nonce state for that account and disrupting subsequent transaction replay for other users sharing the same context?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Medium: Impacts caused by griefing with no economic damage to any user on the network'] [Function: getSenderAccountID()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a request with a sender address that resolves to a hollow account (one where `!account.hasKey() || account.key().equals(IMMUTABILITY_SENTINEL_KEY)`), causing `getSenderAccountID()` to unconditionally write `DEFAULT_KEY` into the `writableAccountCache` via `ContractCallContext.get().getWriteCacheState(AccountReadableKVState.STATE_ID)`, potentially polluting the write cache and causing unexpected behavior for other operations in the same context?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Medium: Impacts caused by griefing with no economic damage to any user on the network'] [Function: buildSuccessResult()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a contract call that produces a large number of failed child transactions, causing `populateChildTransactionErrors()` to collect many error strings and `buildSuccessResult()` to emit a `log.warn()` with a large `childTransactionErrors` collection, resulting in excessive log spam that degrades observability and fills log storage for operators?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Medium: Impacts caused by griefing with no economic damage to any user on the network'] [Function: handleFailedResult()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the opcode context branch in `handleFailedResult()` — where `ContractCallContext.get().getOpcodeContext() != null` causes a failed result to be returned instead of throwing `MirrorEvmTransactionException` — to suppress error propagation and cause callers to silently receive a failed `EvmTransactionResult` without any exception, leading to incorrect downstream behavior?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Medium: Impacts caused by griefing with no economic damage to any user on the network'] [Function: execute()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request where `params instanceof ContractDebugParameters` is true and `debugParams.getEthereumData()` is a non-empty byte array that is malformed (not valid RLP), causing `populateEthTxData()` inside `patchSenderNonce()` to throw an unchecked exception that propagates up through `execute()` and results in an unhandled 500 error, disrupting service for the requesting user?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Medium: Impacts caused by griefing with no economic damage to any user on the network'] [Function: defaultTransactionBodyBuilder()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `transactionValidStart` is set to `Instant.now().getEpochSecond()` with nanoseconds hardcoded to 0, combined with the fixed `TRANSACTION_DURATION = new Duration(15)`, to craft timing-based requests that consistently land at the boundary of the 15-second validity window and cause the executor to reject transactions as expired, griefing the service's reliability?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Medium: Impacts caused by griefing with no economic damage to any user on the network'] [Function: getSenderAccountIDAsNum()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a request with a non-long-zero sender address that does not exist in `aliasesReadableKVState`, causing `aliasesReadableKVState.get(convertAddressToProtoBytes(senderAddress))` to return null and `throwPayerAccountNotFoundException()` to be called, generating a `MirrorEvmTransactionException(PAYER_ACCOUNT_NOT_FOUND, ...)` that is surfaced to the caller — and by doing this at high frequency, can the attacker flood the error-handling pipeline and degrade response quality for legitimate users?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Medium: Impacts caused by griefing with no economic damage to any user on the network'] [Function: buildContractCallTransactionBody()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a `params.getCallData()` byte array of maximum allowed size to `Bytes.wrap(params.getCallData())` inside `buildContractCallTransactionBody()`, causing the `functionParameters` field of the `ContractCallTransactionBody` to be set to a very large value and forcing the executor to process an oversized transaction body, consuming disproportionate CPU and memory?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Medium: Impacts caused by griefing with no economic damage to any user on the network'] [Function: execute()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request where `params.getReceiver().isZero()` is true (triggering contract create path) but `params.getCallData()` is empty or null, causing `buildContractCreateTransactionBody()` to wrap an empty/null byte array via `Bytes.wrap(params.getCallData())` and submit a malformed `initcode` to the executor, resulting in a confusing error response that misleads the caller?
